title: $keyvalidation
text: $keyvalidation Identify whether a primary or candidate key requires validation. $keyvalidation ( Entity , KeyNumber ) Example: if ($keyvalidation ("CUSTOMER", 1) > 0) ... Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. KeyNumber Numeric Key identifier; the value will be truncated to form an integer. 1, the primary key. 2, 3, 4, and so on, the number that identifies a candidate key that has been defined for Entity . Return Values Table 2. Values returned in $keyvalidation Value Meaning "" An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error. 0 No validation is required. 1 Key requires validation. Table 3. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following this Function Value Error constant Meaning -1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not present in the component structure. -1104 <UPROCERR_KEY> The specified key is not valid; for example, the key number was out of range. -1128 <UPROCERR_NOT_A_KEY> The specified key is an index. Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Description A key needs validation in either of the following circumstances: Data in one of the fields that makes up the key has been modified ( $keymod is 1), but has not yet been successfully validated ( $keyvalidation is also 1). $keycheck is 1, regardless of the value of $keyvalidation . Example: Validating Keys The following example uses the $keyvalidation function: ; for each key ; if validation is pending, ; do it ; uh-oh ; next key, please vEntity = "CUSTOMER" vKey = 1 while ( vKey <= $totkeys(vEntity )) if ($keyvalidation (vEntity , vKey ) > 0) validatekey vEntity , vKey if ($status < 0) return -1 endif endif vKey = vKey + 1 endwhile Copy Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). validatefield Validate a field of the current occurrence. validatekey Validate a key of the current occurrence. validateocc Validate an occurrence. $occvalidation Check whether an occurrence requires validation.
